stories.description: Simulation Accuracy Micro-Fixes: correlation diagonal, graduated garbage time, spread confidence ceiling

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: get_correlation("team_win", "team_win", same_game=False) returns exactly 0.15
get_correlation("team_win", "team_win", same_game=True) returns exactly 1.0
correlated_parlay_prob([0.6, 0.6], ["team_win", "team_win"], seed=42) returns a value in [0.30, 0.42] (not inflated to 0.60)
quarter_intensity(4, 31.0) returns a value between 0.77 and 0.79 (1.08 * 0.72 graduated tier)
quarter_intensity(3, 31.0) returns a value between 0.72 and 0.75 (1.02 * 0.72), confirming Q3 blowout trigger activates at margin >= 30
quarter_intensity(4, 16.0) returns a value between 1.02 and 1.04 (1.08 * 0.95, the 15-19 tier)
_spread_confidence_cap(0.75, 13.5) returns 0.50 (hard cap for abs_spread >= 13.0)
_spread_confidence_cap(0.75, 10.5) returns 0.52 (cap for abs_spread in [10.0, 13.0))
_spread_confidence_cap(0.58, 6.0) returns 0.58 (no cap for abs_spread < 8.0)
All existing test_correlations.py tests pass (minimum 9 tests)
All existing test_outcome_model.py tests pass (minimum 8 tests)
correlations.py does NOT contain any code path where get_correlation() returns 1.0 when same_game=False and i != j in correlated_parlay_prob

stories.description: Operational Safety Rails: started-game gate and parlay leg cap

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: is_game_started(g, now_et) returns True when tipoff_et is 10 minutes before now_et
is_game_started(g, now_et) returns False when tipoff_et is 30 minutes after now_et
_classify_parlay(4) returns 'STANDARD', _classify_parlay(5) returns 'HIGH_RISK', _classify_parlay(7) returns 'LOTTERY'
evaluate_mg_parlay(results, legs) with len(legs) == 5 produces a ParlayResult where is_lottery_grade == True
evaluate_mg_parlay(results, legs) with len(legs) == 3 produces a ParlayResult where is_lottery_grade == False
run_tonight.py main() loop excludes started games from simulation â€” is_game_started() is called before simulate_game() for each game in games_to_run

stories.description: Per-Sim Game-State Tracking: vectorize pace engine and quarter intensity, remove median-diff scalar

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: predict_period_possessions_vec(100.0, 100.0, 4, np.array([-30.0, 0.0, 30.0])) returns array of shape (3,) where element 0 (large trailing) has MORE possessions than element 2 (large leading)
quarter_intensity_vec(4, np.array([-31.0, 0.0, 31.0])) returns array of shape (3,) where elements 0 and 2 are less than element 1 (blowout paths slower than close game)
quarter_intensity_vec(3, np.array([-31.0, 0.0, 31.0])) returns array where blowout elements (0 and 2) have a value < 0.76 (Q3 blowout trigger at 30+ applies)
simulate_game(home, away, n_sims=50_000) with home ortg=130 drtg=100 vs away ortg=100 drtg=130 produces np.std(result.margin) > 12.0 (blowout tail now present, was suppressed by median-diff)
simulate_game() with equal teams (both ortg=115, drtg=115) still produces 90 < np.mean(home_scores) < 130 (distribution sanity preserved)
simulate_game() with equal teams produces zero ties after OT resolution at n_sims=100_000
All 8 existing test_pace_engine.py scalar tests pass
simulator.py does NOT contain np.median(home_scores - away_scores) anywhere after the fix

gateCmds.unit: python -m pytest test_pace_engine.py test_outcome_model.py test_quarter_sim.py test_correlated_quarters.py -v --tb=short

stories.description: Probability Calibration Layer: calibration.py module with spread underdog cap and uncertainty haircuts

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: spread_underdog_cap(0.70, 13.5) returns exactly 0.50
spread_underdog_cap(0.70, 10.5) returns exactly 0.52
spread_underdog_cap(0.58, 5.0) returns 0.58 (no cap for abs_spread < 7.5)
haircut_prob(0.60, 10_000, k=1.5) returns a value between 0.585 and 0.595
haircut_prob(0.0, 10_000) returns 0.0 (floor enforced, no negative probabilities)
run_tonight.py imports and calls both spread_underdog_cap() and haircut_prob() in the cover-probability recommendation path (the Phase 1 _spread_confidence_cap() helper is removed)
All existing test_market_blender.py tests still pass (minimum 8 tests)

stories.description: Non-Linear Injury Stacking: stacking multiplier and n_key_out field on GameEntry

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: injury_stacking_multiplier(0) returns 1.0
injury_stacking_multiplier(4) returns 1.45
_make_teams(g) where g.away_n_key_out = 4 and g.away_injury_adj = 7.5 produces an away TeamInput where the effective ortg reduction exceeds 10.5 points
GameEntry dataclass has home_n_key_out: int = 0 and away_n_key_out: int = 0 with defaults (existing code that doesn't pass these args still works)
All existing tests that instantiate GameEntry or call _make_teams() continue to pass

stories.description: Automated Injury & Stats Feed: ESPN scraper + nba_api last-10-game ratings + recency weighting

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: fetch_injuries('LAL') returns an empty list (not an exception) when the ESPN endpoint returns a network error
fetch_last10_ratings(['LAL', 'NOP']) returns an empty dict (not an exception) when the nba_api call fails
_make_teams(g) where g.home_last10_ortg = 120.0 and g.home_ortg = 115.0 produces a home TeamInput with an effective ortg contribution of 118.0 (recency-weighted: 0.60*120 + 0.40*115)
_make_teams(g) where g.home_last10_ortg = None produces identical result to a pre-Phase-6 _make_teams() call (backward compat)

GameEntry dataclass accepts all six last10 fields (home_last10_ortg, home_last10_drtg, home_last10_pace, away_last10_ortg, away_last10_drtg, away_last10_pace) as optional float | None with default None
update_with_live_ratings() returns {success: False, coverage: 0.0} when both feeds fail, without raising an exception
requirements.txt includes nba_api as a dependency

stories.description: Nightly Calibration & Backtest Report: SQLite predictions log, actuals ingestion, Brier/log-loss report

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: log_prediction('2026-03-03', 'SAS@PHI', 'spread_away', 7.5, 0.75, 0.62, 6.5) followed by log_actual('2026-03-03', 'SAS@PHI', 131, 91) produces a BacktestReport entry where the bet is scored as a LOSS (PHI +7.5 did not cover SAS 40-point win)
Brier score of a prediction where model_prob=1.0 and outcome=1 is 0.0
backtest_report() returns separate Brier/log-loss rows for 'spread' and 'total' market types
run_tonight.py calls log_prediction() for each recommendation without raising an exception when calibration_log is unavailable (import wrapped in try/except)
log_results.py --date 2026-03-03 --scores 'CHA 117 DAL 90' inserts an actuals row with home_score=117, away_score=90 for game_id matching DAL@CHA
predictions.db is created in the project root and predictions.db is listed in .gitignore
